title: Version currency
summary: Every new build adopts the current stable release of every language runtime, framework, and top-level dependency. Inherited defaults and template-shipped versions are not acceptable. Stale dependencies and EOL runtimes are rejected at build time. This policy prevents shipping a greenfield project on a 3-major-version-old typescript or an EOL python.

rules.summary: Manifest entries must be within one major version of current stable. Entries more than one major behind require an inline `@pin <reason>` annotation adjacent to the dependency.
Language runtimes at end-of-life are rejected regardless of @pin annotations. EOL is EOL.
The intake brief's `constraints.language_versions` names the primary language version. Briefs that name an EOL version are rejected before any work starts.
Version currency reports are reviewed for known CVEs in stale majors, abandoned upstreams, and license drift. Findings block release independently of @pin annotations.
